morning.notes: Basho found something in the bushes on the north loop. Probably a rabbit. We watched it for five minutes. Good start.

work.notes: Closed a renewal with Hartwick Solutions — a medium one, $84K ARR. My manager sent a Slack message. I said thanks. Felt nothing in particular about it, which is normal for renewals. The new logo hunt is more interesting but also where I keep stalling.
work.commission: If the current pipeline closes at 70%, I'll clear $2,800 extra this quarter. All of it goes to the card.

debt.notes: Did the Sunday review on Tuesday this week. Still counts. $26,400. Target is under $22K by end of Q3. Math works if the pipeline closes.

camera.notes: Looked at it again tonight. Did not pick it up. The project I keep almost defining: I want to photograph the coffee shop on Saturday mornings — the same table, different people, over a year. Simple. I have been 'almost defining' this for two months.

portland.notes: Marcus texted: 'you coming or what.' I said I'd book it this weekend. I will book it this weekend.

evening.reading: Picked up the Bashō collection again. Read three pages. Put it down. That's fine.

at_risk: Camera project still only defined in my head
notes: The 'okay' days are good. I know that now. I spent a year waiting for something to be better than okay before I could start doing the things I wanted to do. That was wrong.
